title: Frontend Batch 37 - Full-Suite Stability and E2E Readiness Reinforcement
summary: Added deterministic e2e readiness checks and stabilized the full Vitest command by bounding threads workers.
testInfrastructureFindings: Unbounded Vitest worker startup could fail the run under WSL load even after test files had passed.
Switching to the forks pool worsened worker startup failures.
Keeping the threads pool and bounding workers to 4 allowed the full suite to pass.

validation.command: npx skills find vitest playwright e2e readiness stability docker
validation.notes: Results were general Playwright/e2e skills; no new skill was installed.
RED failed before helper implementation. Final focused run: 1 file, 5 tests.
Docker daemon, backend health URL, and frontend URL are unavailable in the current environment; output is sanitized.
Worker-bound focused stability run: 2 files, 17 tests.

remainingGaps: Full Playwright e2e still needs Docker Desktop WSL integration or an already running local stack.
The known jsdom navigation warning remains to be isolated.
Full Vitest now passes but takes roughly 14 minutes with bounded workers.
recommendation: Proceed to Frontend Batch 38 - JSDOM Navigation Warning Isolation.
